# Dia Browser Security Page - Content Extraction

## Main Sections

**The Browser Company Bounty Program**
- Bug bounty program managed through HackerOne
- Link: https://hackerone.com/bcny
- Focus: Security and privacy vulnerabilities in BCNY products
- Contact: help@diabrowser.com for security questions

**Keeping Dia Secure**

Core security approaches include:

1. **Outside Security Assessments**
   - Annual full-scope assessments with independent firms
   - Point-in-time reviews for new/high-risk features
   - Code reviews, architecture analysis, exploit testing
   - Notable findings published in Security Bulletins

2. **Browser Engine**
   - Built on Chromium (same as Chrome/Edge)
   - Weekly updates every Thursday with latest stable Chromium
   - Critical security fixes within 48 hours of Google release

3. **Disabled Chromium Features**
   - Google Accounts Integration (GAIA)
   - Google metrics (UMA) reporting
   - Profile settings upload after reset
   - Reporting Observers and API
   - Network logging to file

4. **Infrastructure**
   - Role-based production access restrictions
   - Data encryption at rest and in transit
   - Minimal data retention policies
   - Regular access audits

## FAQ Highlights

**Prompt Injection Defense:** "Dia won't open or follow LLM-generated URLs" and implements layered controls limiting blast radius

**Data Storage:** Conversations stored locally encrypted; minimum data sent to trusted AI partners

**Data Training:** De-identified content retained 30 days maximum; model providers contractually prohibited from retaining data

**AI Content Flagging:** Security/compliance data retained only as long as needed for abuse detection and legal obligations

**Ad-Blocker:** Built-in, controllable via Settings > Privacy
